title: BSP Andes DMA adapter applicability audit
question: Does the Orange Pi/A733 BSP Andes DMA adapter explain why vendor SDMMC0 IDMAC can fetch descriptors while mainline cannot?
test_cost: low; source and live config audit only, no kernel build and no board reboot

inputs.cubie3_vendor_config: CONFIG_AW_ANDES_DMA_ADAPT is not set; CONFIG_ARM64=y; CONFIG_DMA_CMA=y

observations: The BSP has bsp/drivers/andes_adaptor/dma_ops_adapter.c, exporting aw_andes_dma_ops only when CONFIG_AW_ANDES_DMA_OPS is enabled.
The vendor Cubie3 running config reports CONFIG_AW_ANDES_DMA_ADAPT is not set.
Source search found no SDMMC0, ARM64, or platform path assigning aw_andes_dma_ops to the MMC device.
The adapter assumes a no-IOMMU contiguous-memory platform and appears unrelated to the ARM64 Cubie A7S SDMMC0 path actually under test.

decision.summary: The Andes DMA adapter is not active in the vendor Cubie3 ARM64 kernel and is not wired to SDMMC0. It is not a credible explanation for the descriptor-fetch difference.
decision.next_step: Do not port or emulate aw_andes_dma_ops for A733 SDMMC0. Continue looking only for active vendor/runtime handoff or security/fabric evidence.
